title: dracocephalum subcapitatum
intro: Dracocephalum subcapitatum, also known as Tufted Dragonhead or Western Dragonhead, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds such as volatile oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Tufted Dragonhead include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues like bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Tufted Dragonhead are typically made through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and can be used internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
